sampled_assertions.reading_text: Computation is any process that takes information in, applies a defined set of steps to it, and produces a result.
Input — the raw information going in. Process — the defined steps. Output — the result.
the steps are specific and unambiguous — precise instructions that leave no room for guessing
Vague: 'Add a pinch of salt.' Defined: 'Add exactly 2 grams of salt.'
Computable means: there exists a finite set of defined steps that always produces the correct result in a finite amount of time
recognising speech, identifying objects in photos — are now solved by modern AI (Artificial Intelligence) systems [2][3]
Traffic light on a timer: Input: time elapsed... Process: if time ≥ green_duration, switch to amber...
Vending machine: check whether inserted value ≥ item price AND item is in stock
a bank card transaction check... typically finishing in under 200 milliseconds [2][3]
Modern AI systems... take inputs (your words, an image, your click history), apply sequences of defined steps, and produce outputs [2][3]
